recent_trades.summary: Adding AVGO on strong AI-related news, positive sentiment, and consistent upward price momentum over the past month.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to NVDA on 7-day win streak, very positive sentiment, and AI momentum that remains unhindered by macro headlines.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a starter position in AAPL on strong positive sentiment and valuation support.
recent_trades.reasoning: Apple is seeing highly positive news flow highlighting it as a strong buy relative to its peers. Opening a small position provides defensive tech exposure while keeping total open positions at the maximum limit of 10.

recent_trades.summary: Doubling our XOM allocation to serve as a strategic hedge against Middle East geopolitical risks.
recent_trades.reasoning: Macro headlines point to escalating conflict with Iran and potential disruptions to global oil traffic. Increasing our exposure to ExxonMobil directly hedges the broader portfolio against an oil price spike driven by these geopolitical tensions.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating META to reallocate capital away from negative sentiment drivers.
recent_trades.reasoning: Meta is facing negative news regarding recent lawsuit supply lines and currently holds the lowest sentiment among our mega-cap tech holdings. Exiting this position frees up both capital and a portfolio slot for more strategic defensive plays.

recent_trades.summary: Topping up Nvidia as the stock extends a strong multi-day run with the best sentiment and a clean test of recent highs.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA has the strongest sentiment in the group, a 7-day winning streak mention, and price is sitting essentially at its 30-day high with clear intraday relative strength. The stock is already a core holding, so a small increase from 19.03% to the 20% cap concentrates capital in a leading momentum name while staying within risk limits.

recent_trades.summary: Adding slightly to Broadcom on continued AI-related strength, positive news flow, and price action pressing fresh 30-day highs.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO has among the best technical setups in the universe, with strong 5-day and 30-day performance and price trading just under its 30-day high while the intraday candles remain constructive above the SMA. Positive AI-stock headlines reinforce the trend, and moving from 19.04% to the 20% cap is a measured add rather than an aggressive chase.

recent_trades.summary: Closing JPM because the bank setup is flat, news tone is mildly negative, and the small position offers limited upside versus higher-momentum alternatives.

Since the position is only 1.99% of the portfolio, rotating out simplifies the book and creates room for additions in stronger trends.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Walmart as defensive retail is drifting lower and the position is too small to justify keeping while stronger opportunities lead today.

At 2.49% weight it contributes little to performance, so freeing this slot and cash improves flexibility for stronger names.

recent_trades.summary: Adding AVGO on strong momentum (+1.4% today, +1.49% 5D), positive AI tailwinds, and billionaire Ken Fisher backing as a top AI pick for 2026.

Using proceeds from HD sale to establish an AVGO position at ~12% weight fits within the 20% cap and diversifies into semiconductors alongside NVDA. The Ken Fisher endorsement and AI cloud computing tailwinds (CoreWeave/Anthropic) support the thesis.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting HD as it's the weakest position with negative momentum, Jim Cramer calling it one of his worst stocks, and neutral-to-negative news flow.

Adding Exxon Mobil introduces vital energy exposure to the portfolio, acting as a defensive buffer against energy supply shocks and negative broader market sentiment.

recent_trades.summary: Adding slightly to NVDA on strong momentum, very positive sentiment, and price pressing the top of its recent range, confidence 8/10.

universe.description: Default 20-name universe: mega-cap leaders across all major sectors. Liquid, well-covered, large-cap U.S. equities.
